description_long: This is a goblet-shaped drum with a wide, circular, off-white drumhead made of what appears to be animal skin or synthetic material. The body is a deep reddish-brown, likely wooden or synthetic, and tapers inward before flaring out at the base, giving it a stable stance. Silver or gray tension rods or ropes run vertically from the drumhead to the base, providing structural support and tuning capability. The drumhead is tightly stretched and slightly textured, while the body has a matte, slightly rough finish. There are no visible handles, labels, or decorative markings on the exterior. The overall appearance is clean and well-maintained, suggesting it is either new or only lightly used.
description: A goblet-shaped drum with a reddish-brown body, off-white drumhead, and silver tension rods. The surface is clean and the drum appears well-maintained.
description_view_0: The drum is seen from a slightly angled front, showing the full drumhead and the vertical tension rods clearly.
description_view_1: This view shows the drum from a slightly rotated angle, highlighting the smooth, flared base and the even spacing of the tension rods.
description_view_2: The drum is viewed from another side, with the drumhead and body proportions consistent and the rods symmetrically arranged.
description_view_3: This view presents the drum from the opposite side, again showing the clean drumhead and the regular pattern of the tension rods.
